methods.description: The analogues method is an empirical approach to forecast weather and predict natural climate evolution by identifying analogous synoptic situations in meteorological archives. It involves comparing past climate states and forcing conditions to those of the Holocene to find similar insolation measures.

steps.description: Seek analogous synoptic situations to today's in the archives of meteorological services.
steps.searchable_summary: retrieve historical synoptic weather data from meteorological archives

steps.description: Identify in palaeoclimate archives situations for which the state of climate and the forcing conditions were similar to those experienced during the Holocene.
steps.searchable_summary: compare palaeoclimate data to Holocene conditions

steps.description: Consider specific measures of insolation and look at past times where that specific measure of insolation was the same as during the Holocene.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze insolation measures for historical matches

steps.description: Evaluate the timing of insolation matches and their correlation with climate changes.
steps.searchable_summary: validate insolation match timing against climate records

methods.description: The V−D model is a conceptual model used to simulate climate history by integrating astronomical forcing with a correction factor. It involves using a synthetic mix of obliquity and precession to model the total volume of continental ice and ocean circulation states.

steps.description: Force the system with a synthetic mix of obliquity and precession.
steps.searchable_summary: apply synthetic obliquity and precession forcing to model

steps.description: Integrate the forcing with a correction D that shifts between two states according to a hysteresis behavior controlled by V.
steps.searchable_summary: integrate forcing with hysteresis correction in model

steps.description: Superimpose V to the benthic stack by Lisiecki and Raymo (2005) to highlight the similarity between the simulation and the palaeoclimate record.
steps.searchable_summary: compare model output to benthic stack data

steps.description: Add a small stochastic forcing to D and observe the phase shift around −400 ka.
steps.searchable_summary: introduce stochastic forcing to model and observe phase shift
